To find the range where the button area is at least 10 square inches, we set up the inequality 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 ≥ 10, which simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 ≥ 0. Evaluating the polynomial at the boundaries of the interval [3, 6], we find P(3) = 2(27) - 5(9) + 3(3) - 7 = 54 - 45 + 9 - 7 = 11 and P(6) = 2(216) - 5(36) + 3(6) - 7 = 432 - 180 + 18 - 7 = 263. Since the derivative P'(x) = 6x^2 - 10x + 3 is positive for all x ≥ 3 (as the roots are approximately 0.4 and 1.2), the function is strictly increasing in the interval [3, 6]. Because the minimum value in this interval is 11, which is greater than 10, the inequality holds for the entire range. Thus, the valid range of x-values is 3 ≤ x ≤ 6.

******

To find the screen width where the area is exactly 15 square inches, we solve the equation 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15, which simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0. Testing integer values, we find that x = 3 yields 54 - 45 + 9 - 22 = -4 and x = 4 yields 128 - 80 + 12 - 22 = 38, indicating a root between 3 and 4. Using numerical approximation methods, we determine the real root is approximately x ≈ 3.23 inches. Since the cubic equation has one real root and two complex conjugate roots, the only physical screen width that satisfies the specification is approximately 3.23 inches.
